title: Codex Review

codex.stdout:
The patch currently introduces a build-breaking unused local in `checkAndCostUserBenefitsForSkills`. Beyond that, the new skill-benefit and skill-editing flows contain regressions that can bypass paywalls, crash on stale references, and corrupt edited skill packages.

Full review comments:

- [P0] Remove unused `indexToSkillKey` local — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_route.go:1515-1515
  This local is never read, so `go build`/`go test` for `service/page/chat` will fail with `declared and not used: indexToSkillKey`. The new skill-benefit path cannot ship until this variable is removed or actually consumed.

- [P1] Run general benefit checks for skill-key items — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_benefits.go:552-554
  When `item.SkillKey` is present we only enqueue the item for `checkSkillBenefitBatchInternal`; it never goes through `CheckUserBenefitsBatch`. In `checkAndCostUserBenefitsForSkills` every referenced/auto-invoked skill sets `SkillKey`, so any paywall modeled through `SubOfficeID` (for example pro-mode or advanced-agent benefit items) is neither checked nor charged, and those skills become usable as long as `min_membership_type` passes.

- [P1] Fail closed when skill benefit checks error — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_route.go:1532-1538
  If `CheckSkillBenefitBatch` hits a transient DB/Marketing error, or if its response length is inconsistent, this branch returns `true` and leaves all skills at their default `IsBenefitValid=true`. `injectSkillInfo` then forwards the referenced skills to the agent anyway, so premium skills are exposed during backend failures instead of being rejected or filtered out.

- [P2] Guard missing legacy-sub-office skill lookups — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_sender.go:9352-9358
  `FindByLegacySubOfficeID` returns `(nil, nil)` when the legacy id is unknown. In that case `skill.SkillKey` dereferences a nil pointer and crashes the request. A stale or deleted `legacy_sub_office_id` inside `<reference>` content is enough to trigger this path.

- [P2] Preserve auxiliary files when editing a skill archive — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/file_artifact_update_sender.go:991-1002
  This rebuilds the edited archive from scratch and writes only `SKILL.md`. For any skill package that also contains `scripts/`, `assets/`, templates, or other files, editing the markdown produces a new zip that silently drops those dependencies, so the next invocation of the skill breaks even though the user only changed metadata or instructions.

rendered:
# Codex Review

Target: branch diff against master

The patch currently introduces a build-breaking unused local in `checkAndCostUserBenefitsForSkills`. Beyond that, the new skill-benefit and skill-editing flows contain regressions that can bypass paywalls, crash on stale references, and corrupt edited skill packages.

Full review comments:

- [P0] Remove unused `indexToSkillKey` local — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_route.go:1515-1515
  This local is never read, so `go build`/`go test` for `service/page/chat` will fail with `declared and not used: indexToSkillKey`. The new skill-benefit path cannot ship until this variable is removed or actually consumed.

- [P1] Run general benefit checks for skill-key items — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_benefits.go:552-554
  When `item.SkillKey` is present we only enqueue the item for `checkSkillBenefitBatchInternal`; it never goes through `CheckUserBenefitsBatch`. In `checkAndCostUserBenefitsForSkills` every referenced/auto-invoked skill sets `SkillKey`, so any paywall modeled through `SubOfficeID` (for example pro-mode or advanced-agent benefit items) is neither checked nor charged, and those skills become usable as long as `min_membership_type` passes.

- [P1] Fail closed when skill benefit checks error — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_route.go:1532-1538
  If `CheckSkillBenefitBatch` hits a transient DB/Marketing error, or if its response length is inconsistent, this branch returns `true` and leaves all skills at their default `IsBenefitValid=true`. `injectSkillInfo` then forwards the referenced skills to the agent anyway, so premium skills are exposed during backend failures instead of being rejected or filtered out.

- [P2] Guard missing legacy-sub-office skill lookups — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/chat_sender.go:9352-9358
  `FindByLegacySubOfficeID` returns `(nil, nil)` when the legacy id is unknown. In that case `skill.SkillKey` dereferences a nil pointer and crashes the request. A stale or deleted `legacy_sub_office_id` inside `<reference>` content is enough to trigger this path.

- [P2] Preserve auxiliary files when editing a skill archive — /Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/chat/service/page/chat/file_artifact_update_sender.go:991-1002
  This rebuilds the edited archive from scratch and writes only `SKILL.md`. For any skill package that also contains `scripts/`, `assets/`, templates, or other files, editing the markdown produces a new zip that silently drops those dependencies, so the next invocation of the skill breaks even though the user only changed metadata or instructions.
